summary: Iteration 4 — frontend-only regression after SortablePage extraction (components/SortablePage.jsx) and Library.jsx IIFE+stable-key refactor. All critical flows verified end-to-end against the public REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. No regressions found.

issues: Pre-existing a11y warning still present: 'Missing Description or aria-describedby={undefined} for {DialogContent}' (radix-ui). Non-blocking.

action_items: (Optional/a11y, still open from iter 3) Add DialogDescription/aria-describedby to New Flipbook + AlertDialog in Library.jsx and Share dialog in BookEditor.jsx.
(Pre-existing, still open) server.py _reprocess_page records orphaned objects on rotate/crop — verify the orphan cleanup job actually purges them.
(Pre-existing) server.py upload_cover only checks extension — should validate content via Pillow open/re-encode.
critical_code_review_comments: SortablePage extraction is clean: all data-testids preserved (page-thumb-, drag-page-, page-menu-, set-cover-, rotate-page-, crop-page-, delete-page-, cover-badge-). useSortable wired correctly; isDragging styles still applied. No prop-drilling regressions — handlers are passed as callbacks (onSetCover, onRotate, onCrop, onDelete) and BookEditor's state updates still flow through.

Library.jsx IIFE rendering is correct — skeleton uses stable keys ['s1'..'s4'] (no array-index warning), empty-state and grid branches mutually exclusive. Loading→books transition verified (existing 3 books rendered).
BookEditor.jsx import cleanup verified — SortablePage is the only thing imported from components/SortablePage; no dead imports (Trash2/GripVertical/etc. were correctly removed from this file).
dnd-kit drag-reorder with PointerSensor activationConstraint distance=5 still triggers correctly with multi-step mouse moves; reorder persisted across reload via PUT /api/books/:id/reorder.

success_rate.backend: N/A (frontend-only requested; backend last validated 27/27 in iter 3)
success_rate.frontend: 100% — library load + 3 existing book cards, create-with-files (2 images + 3-page PDF → 5 pages), set-cover badge, rotate (src changed), crop dialog + confirm, drag-reorder (last→first) persisted on reload, custom cover upload, page delete (5→4), reader page-indicator '1/4' + spread-toggle, share enable + /s/<shareId> renders read-only (no upload-zone/share-button/custom-cover-button, page-indicator present), share disable + library delete + card removed.

seed_data_creation: Created TEST_Iter4_Refactor book with 5 pages (landscape.png, portrait.png, 3-page multi.pdf); fully cleaned up via UI delete at end of run. /tmp/landscape.png /tmp/portrait.png /tmp/square.png /tmp/multi.pdf generated via PIL+PyMuPDF.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Refactor is regression-free. All testids unchanged. PointerSensor distance=5 requires multi-step pointer moves for drag tests. Share disable check: visiting /s/<id> after disable returns blank/no-page-indicator UI rather than literal '404'/'not available' string — confirm via absence of page-indicator instead of body-text match. Three pre-existing books are present in this preview env library; if testing 'empty-state', delete them first or assert empty-state only on a fresh DB. Reader correctly hides upload-zone/share-flipbook-button/custom-cover-button on /s/ route.
rca of the issue: No bugs found. Only minor test-assertion imprecision noted: 'share disabled' was checked by searching body text for 'not'/'unavailable'/'404' which returned False — the SPA route /s/<id> on a disabled share renders a quiet empty state (no page-indicator) instead of an error string. Functional behavior is correct (verified by absence of reader controls).
